title: Having Harmonious Devotee Relationships | Sri Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa | SB 3.21.48 | 11.12.2025
speaker: HG Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa

segments.text: Translation and purport by His Divine Grace A. C. Bhattivedanta Swami Srila Prabhupada
Seeing that the monarch had come to his hermitage and was bowing before him,
the sage treated him with benediction
and received him as his son.
He blessed him with due honor.

How we should deal with others, particularly while preaching, it is said that we should approach them with all humility, glorify them, and then when they are a little softened, and then we say, Sarchant Hare Krishna.

This should be our process. We should not think, I know much better than him, I know the highest philosophy, I am serving supreme personality of Godhead, I am a servant of him, and then, what is he? He is serving Maya.
You know, he is, you know, all kinds of, so, that kind of a behavior will alienate people.
They will think, what is this, you know, this person is so, you know, so rough.
So that's why each one of us should learn the way to approach others, and this is the technique given by Prabodhananda Saraswati.
When we go and preach, because they are all, they feel that they are very, very exalted, very advanced.
So when we go to them, we should actually appreciate them.

Appreciate their current position, appreciate that little devotion that they are showing, at least he has allowed me to come and enter into his house, appreciate, and then slowly you introduce Krishna Consciousness.

our behavior should be in such a way that that person is not alienated, doesn't become inimical towards us, towards the movement, but rather he becomes very appreciative.

Then, you know, other disciples, they made arrangements, and then he came.
He comes to Sri Prabhupada and says, immediately pays obeisances and says, I wanted to see you, because yesterday one of your disciples came to me,
and the way he spoke about you and the Krishna Consciousness is so wonderful, I decided, I want to come and see you.
So, when we go and come out from that house, they should feel, yes, I should come to Hare Krishna Mandir.
Not that, oh God, someone will come from Hare Krishna Mandir, he has come.
So, we should enhance the prestige of the institution, of the spiritual master.
So then, it becomes very easy for us to convince him to take the next steps, highest steps in Krishna Consciousness.
So, these are the various angles when we are dealing with seniors and outsiders.

or they are having a different philosophy, the tendency is to criticize their philosophies and criticize their worship.

he gets such a higher philosophy, from a high point, he can see mistakes of every other thing, every other philosophy in this world.

what is wrong and what is not correct, he comes to know.
But that doesn't give us a license to criticize and minimize that person, and he becomes inimical and alienated from us.
Our mood should be to build bridge with that person.

You know, you are like this, you are like that, we can do that.
